keyTerms.Bruce Lee: Hong Kong-American martial artist and actor whose 1973 film Enter the Dragon launched kung fu cinema globally
keyTerms.Shaw Brothers: Hong Kong film studio that produced hundreds of martial arts films from the 1950s to 1980s, defining the genre
keyTerms.wire-fu: A filmmaking technique using wire harnesses to create the illusion of superhuman acrobatics and flight in fight scenes
keyTerms.Yuen Woo-ping: Hong Kong action choreographer who brought wire-fu to Hollywood through films like The Matrix and Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon
keyTerms.Wudang Mountain: A sacred Daoist site in Hubei province, legendary birthplace of internal martial arts including tai chi
